text:
🤖 Agent Z Smart Response (Conversational)
“No problem, I can help you identify this 👍
A terraced house is part of a row of similar houses sharing side walls.A semi-detached house is attached to one other house.A detached house stands alone.A flat or maisonette is part of a building with shared or separate access.
To help you better, could you tell me:👉 Does your property share walls with neighbours on one or both sides?”
If user says:
“Both sides” → Suggest: Terraced
“One side” → Suggest: Semi-detached
“No sides” → Suggest: Detached
“Inside a building” → Suggest: Flat
🤖 Final Confirmation
“Based on your answer, this appears to be a [Suggested Type].Would you like me to select this for you?”
✔ Yes → Auto-fill✔ No → Let user choose manually

AGENT X (ACTION LAYER)
Property Type: Terraced (Confidence: High / Medium if guided)Actions:✔ Proceed with standard HMO workflow✔ Assume shared walls → consider neighbour impact (planning)✔ Prioritise fire escape compliance (mid-terrace risk)✔ Check extension constraints (rear only likely)
If selected via “Unsure”:
Flag: Property Type AI-InferredAction: Verify during document review or floor plan stage

🤖 Agent Z Smart Response (Conversational)
“No worries, I’ll help you figure this out 👍
If you own the property and the land it stands on, you are a freeholder.If you own the property but not the land (usually in flats), you are a leaseholder.If the property is owned by a company, select company-owned.If you’re renting or managing on behalf of someone else, select tenant or acting on behalf of owner.
👉 Do you own the property yourself?”
Guided Follow-up Logic
“Yes, and I own the land” → Freehold
“Yes, but not the land” → Leasehold
“No, I manage it” → Acting on behalf
“I rent it” → Tenant
“Owned via company” → Company-owned
🤖 Final Confirmation
“Based on your answer, this appears to be [Ownership Type].Shall I select this for you?”
✔ Yes → Auto-fill✔ No → Manual selection

AGENT X (ACTION LAYER)
Example Output:
Ownership: LeaseholdConfidence: HighActions:✔ Request Freeholder Consent✔ Check lease restrictions (HMO allowed?)✔ Flag legal dependency before submission✔ Do not proceed to submission without consent
If “Unsure” (AI-assisted):
Ownership: Likely Leasehold (AI Inferred)Confidence: MediumAction:✔ Verify via title deed / land registry✔ Request documentation from client
If Tenant:
Ownership: TenantActions:❌ Cannot proceed independently✔ Request landlord authorization✔ Pause workflow until approval

field: Are you planning any building works ?
text:
🤖 Agent Z Smart Response (Conversational)
“That’s absolutely fine, I’ll help you understand 👍
Building works include any physical changes to your property such as:• Extending the property (rear or side)• Converting loft or garage• Moving or removing walls• Adding bedrooms or bathrooms
👉 Are you planning to change the layout or size of the property in any way?”
Guided Follow-up Logic
“Yes, changing layout” → Internal changes
“Extending house” → Rear extension
“Using loft” → Loft conversion
“Just adding bathroom” → Internal + plumbing
“No changes” → No works
🤖 Final Confirmation
“Based on your answer, this appears to be [Type of Work].Would you like me to select this for you?”
✔ Yes → Auto-fill✔ No → Manual selection
Specific Triggers:
Rear extension → Planning / Permitted Development check
Loft conversion → Fire safety + escape route impact
Internal changes → HMO layout compliance check
Additional bathroom → Licensing improvement
💡 AGENT Z SMART INSIGHT
Example: Rear Extension
“A rear extension may require planning permission or fall under permitted development. We’ll assess this based on your property and location.”
Example: Internal Changes
“Internal layout changes can affect HMO compliance, especially room sizes and fire safety. We’ll review this for you.”
Example: No Changes
“That’s helpful — we’ll assess your existing layout for HMO suitability without structural changes.”

field: Are you planning any building works ?
text:
👩‍💼 AGENT X (ACTION LAYER)
Example Output:

🤖 Agent Z Smart Response (Conversational)
“That’s quite common — I can help 👍
A property is considered extended if any additional space has been added beyond the original structure, such as:• A rear or side extension• A loft conversion with dormer• A conservatory• Any structural addition visible from outside
👉 Does your property have any extra built space compared to neighbouring houses or its original layout?”
🧠 Guided Follow-up Logic
“Yes, at the back” → Rear extension
“Yes, roof changed” → Loft conversion
“Looks bigger than neighbours” → Likely extended
“No change” → No extension
🤖 Final Confirmation
“Based on your answer, this appears to be [Extension Type].Would you like me to select this?”
✔ Yes → Auto-fill✔ No → Manual selection

AGENT X (ACTION LAYER)
Example Output:
Previous Extensions: Rear ExtensionActions:✔ Trigger Planning Constraint Review✔ Check permitted development limits✔ Request existing + proposed drawings✔ Compare with neighbouring properties✔ Flag moderate planning risk
If Multiple Extensions:
Previous Extensions: MultipleActions:🔴 High Planning Sensitivity✔ Full planning route likely✔ Detailed drawings required✔ Escalate for expert review
If “Not Sure”:
Previous Extensions: UnknownActions:✔ Request site photos / floor plans✔ Verify via council records if needed✔ Keep planning status open

🤖 Agent Z Smart Response (Conversational)
“No problem, I’ll help clarify 👍
In an HMO:• Shared facilities mean tenants use the same kitchen or bathroom• Self-contained units mean each tenant has their own kitchen and bathroom
👉 Will your tenants have their own private kitchen and bathroom, or will they share these facilities?”
Guided Follow-up Logic
“Sharing everything” → Shared HMO
“Private bathrooms only” → Ensuite HMO
“Everything separate” → Self-contained (not typical C4)
“Not sure” → Keep open
🤖 Final Confirmation
“Based on your answer, this appears to be [Facility Type].Shall I select this for you?”
✔ Yes → Auto-fill✔ No → Manual selection

AGENT X (ACTION LAYER)
Shared Facilities:
Facility Type: Shared Kitchen + BathroomActions:✔ Confirm HMO classification✔ Trigger licensing workflow✔ Apply standard HMO room + kitchen ratios✔ Proceed with compliance checks
Ensuite Model:
Facility Type: Shared Kitchen + Private BathroomsActions:✔ Maintain HMO classification✔ Adjust bathroom ratio requirements✔ Highlight premium layout (better approval chance)
Self-contained:
Facility Type: Fully Self-containedActions:⚠ Not standard C4 HMO✔ Trigger planning review (possible Sui Generis / flats)✔ Escalate for planning strategy
Not Sure:
Facility Type: UnknownActions:✔ Clarify during consultation✔ Request layout or floor plan✔ Keep classification open

🤖 Agent Z Smart Response (Conversational)
“No problem, I’ll help clarify 👍
This is a key trigger for HMO classification and licensing intent. It helps confirm whether the property operates as a true HMO (room-by-room letting) versus a single tenancy
Renting rooms individually means each tenant has their own agreement and rents a separate room.
Letting the property as a whole means one household (family or group) rents the entire property under a single agreement.
👉 Will your tenants have separate rental agreements for individual rooms?”
Guided Follow-up Logic
“Yes, separate tenants” → Individual letting (HMO)
“One family/group” → Single let (non-HMO)
“Some rooms separate” → Mixed model
“Not sure” → Keep open
🤖 Final Confirmation
“Based on your answer, this appears to be [Letting Type].Would you like me to select this for you?”
✔ Yes → Auto-fill✔ No → Manual selection

AGENT X (ACTION LAYER)
Individual Letting:
Letting Type: Individual RoomsActions:✔ Confirm HMO classification✔ Trigger licensing workflow✔ Apply room-level compliance checks✔ Validate occupancy vs room sizes
Whole Property:
Letting Type: Single TenancyActions:✔ No HMO licence (initial assumption)✔ Verify number of occupants✔ Check if planning still required (if works involved)
Mixed:
Letting Type: MixedActions:⚠ Requires classification review✔ Likely HMO → confirm structure✔ Request tenancy details✔ Escalate for compliance check
Not Sure:
Letting Type: UnknownActions:✔ Clarify during consultation✔ Explain HMO implications✔ Keep classification open

🤖 Agent Z Smart Response (Conversational)
“No problem, I’ll help clarify 👍
A communal kitchen is a shared space where all or multiple tenants prepare and cook food.
If each room has its own private kitchen, then it’s not communal and may be considered self-contained units instead.
👉 Do your tenants use one shared kitchen, or does each have their own cooking space?”
🧠 Guided Follow-up Logic
“Everyone shares one kitchen” → Single communal kitchen
“There are 2 kitchens” → Multiple communal kitchens
“Each room has its own kitchen” → No communal kitchen
“Not sure” → Keep open
🤖 Final Confirmation
“Based on your answer, this appears to be [Kitchen Type].Shall I select this for you?”
✔ Yes → Auto-fill✔ No → Manual selection

AGENT X (ACTION LAYER)
Single Kitchen:
Kitchen Type: Single CommunalActions:✔ Trigger kitchen adequacy check✔ Validate occupants vs kitchen size✔ Ensure appliance standards met✔ Proceed with standard HMO compliance
Multiple Kitchens:
Kitchen Type: Multiple CommunalActions:✔ Distribute occupants across kitchens✔ Improve compliance rating✔ Potential for higher occupancy approval
No Communal Kitchen:
Kitchen Type: NoneActions:⚠ Non-standard HMO✔ Trigger planning classification review✔ Possible self-contained unit model✔ Escalate to planning strategy
Not Sure:
Kitchen Type: UnknownActions:✔ Request floor plan or photos✔ Clarify during consultation✔ Keep classification open

🤖 Agent Z Smart Response (Conversational)
“No problem, I’ll help you with dimensions 👍
Recommended Action: Book a Site Measurement Survey
Because accurate dimensions are required for:
Floor plans
HMO compliance
Kitchen adequacy checks
Planning drawings
Occupancy calculations
Standard Survey Cost (London & Surrounding Areas)
London Zones 1–4: £180 – £250
Greater London / M25 Ring: £220 – £300
Home Counties (Essex, Kent, Surrey, Herts, Berkshire): £250 – £350
(Prices vary based on property size and travel distance.)
Customer Instruction Message
“To proceed accurately, we need verified measurements of your property. Please book a professional site visit and measured survey. This ensures your drawings, compliance checks, and planning documents are correct.”
Zynapsis Button
“Book My Site Survey”
Triggers:
Calendar availability
Payment link
Surveyor assignment

🤖 Agent Z Smart Response (Conversational)
“That’s absolutely fine — I can guide you 👍
The type of wall you choose is important for fire safety, sound insulation, and HMO compliance.
• Brick/Block walls → Strong and durable, but less flexible• Stud walls (plasterboard) → Common for internal layouts, quicker to install• Fire-rated walls → Required in HMOs for safety and compliance
👉 Are you planning to create new rooms or modify the layout?”

AGENT X (ACTION LAYER)
Fire-Rated Walls:
Wall Material: Fire-Rated PartitionsActions:✔ Meets HMO fire compliance✔ Proceed without escalation✔ Align with building regulations
Stud Walls:
Wall Material: Standard StudActions:⚠ Upgrade required for HMO✔ Advise fire-rated specification✔ Flag compliance risk
Existing Walls:
Wall Material: ExistingActions:✔ Conduct fire safety assessment✔ Check door + escape route compliance
Not Decided:
Wall Material: Not DefinedActions:✔ Recommend fire-rated partitions✔ Hold design finalisation✔ Coordinate with Agent Y

🤖 Agent Z Smart Response (Conversational)
“No problem, I’ll guide you 👍
The choice of roof material depends on the type of work you’re planning:
• Tiles or slate → Common for pitched roofs and maintaining the look of the property• Flat roofs (felt/GRP) → Typically used for rear extensions• GRP/Fibreglass → Durable and low maintenance for modern extensions

AGENT X (ACTION LAYER)
Existing Roof:
Roof Material: ExistingActions:✔ No immediate action✔ Verify if loft conversion planned later
Pitched Roof (Tiles/Slate):
Roof Material: Tile/SlateActions:✔ Ensure match with existing structure✔ Check planning aesthetic compliance✔ Proceed with standard validation
Flat Roof:
Roof Material: Flat (GRP/Felt)Actions:✔ Validate extension height limits✔ Check drainage design✔ Align with building regs
Not Decided:
Roof Material: Not DefinedActions:✔ Recommend default (based on project type)✔ Hold design finalisation✔ Coordinate with Agent Y

field: FIELD: Will the new materials match the existing property
text:
🤖 Agent Z Smart Response (Conversational)
“That’s a great question — I can help 👍
In most cases, councils prefer new work to match the existing property, especially for:• Brickwork• Roof tiles• External finishes
This helps the extension or alteration blend in with the original building.

field: FIELD: Will the new materials match the existing property
text:

Match:
Material Match: YesActions:✔ Proceed with planning-friendly design✔ Increase approval probability✔ Continue with standard workflow
Not Matching:

Partial:
Material Match: PartialActions:✔ Review design consistency✔ Flag moderate planning sensitivity✔ Validate with drawings
Not Decided:

field: FIELD: Conservation Area or Near Listed Building
text:
🤖 Agent Z Smart Response (Conversational)
“No problem — I can help you check this 👍
A conservation area is a location where the council protects the character and appearance of buildings.A listed building is officially recognised as historically important and has strict rules for any changes.
👉 If your property is in one of these areas, even small changes may require planning permission.

field: FIELD: Conservation Area or Near Listed Building
text:
AGENT X (ACTION LAYER)
Conservation Area:
Constraint: Conservation AreaActions:🔴 Full Planning Required✔ Enforce material matching✔ Restrict design flexibility✔ Assign experienced planner✔ Increase approval scrutiny level
Near Listed Building:
Constraint: Listed Building ProximityActions:⚠ Design Sensitivity Required✔ Review visual impact✔ Prepare justification if needed
Both:
Constraint: High (Conservation + Listed)Actions:🔴 Specialist Case✔ Escalate to senior planning team✔ Detailed design + heritage consideration✔ No assumptions on permitted development
No Constraints:
Constraint: NoneActions:✔ Proceed with standard workflow✔ Apply general planning rules
Not Sure:
Constraint: UnknownActions:✔ Trigger postcode validation (Z-ynapsis)✔ Do not proceed until confirmed

🤖 Agent Z Smart Response (Conversational)
“No problem — I’ll help you assess this 👍
Trees are important in planning, especially if they are:• Large or mature• Close to your proposed extension• Protected by a Tree Preservation Order (TPO)
👉 A simple way to think about it:If a tree is tall enough to potentially fall onto your proposed structure, it may be relevant.

AGENT X (ACTION LAYER)
Trees Present:
Tree Impact: YesActions:🔴 Trigger Tree Constraint Workflow✔ Check TPO status✔ Request tree survey (if required)✔ Adjust design positioning✔ Inform customer of potential delay
No Trees:
Tree Impact: NoActions:✔ Proceed with standard workflow✔ No environmental restriction
Not Sure:
Tree Impact: UnknownActions:✔ Trigger Z-ynapsis environmental check✔ Request site photos✔ Consider site visit

🤖 Agent Z Smart Response (Conversational)
“No problem — I’ll explain 👍
An Arboricultural Report (BS5837) is a professional report that assesses:• Tree location and condition• Impact of your development on trees• Protection measures during construction
👉 This is usually required if:• There are trees near your development• Trees are protected (TPO)• The council flags tree constraints

AGENT X (ACTION LAYER)
Report Available:
Arboriculture Report: AvailableActions:✔ Attach to documentation briefcase✔ Proceed with planning submission✔ Reduce approval risk
Report NOT Available (Trees Present):

Report NOT Required:
Arboriculture Report: Not RequiredActions:✔ Proceed with standard workflow
Not Sure:
Arboriculture Report: UnknownActions:✔ Verify tree constraint first✔ Request clarification✔ Keep workflow conditional

🤖 Agent Z Smart Response (Conversational)

Flood Zones are defined as:• Zone 1 → Low risk• Zone 2 → Medium risk• Zone 3 → High risk
If your property is in Zone 2 or 3, the council may require a Flood Risk Assessment (FRA) before approval.

Zone 3:
Flood Zone: 3Actions:🔴 Mandatory FRA Required✔ Assign flood risk consultant✔ Adjust design (levels, drainage)✔ Hold submission until report ready

Zone 1:
Flood Zone: 1Actions:✔ No flood constraint✔ Proceed with standard workflow
Not Sure:
Flood Zone: UnknownActions:✔ Trigger Z-ynapsis flood check✔ Confirm before proceeding

🤖 Agent Z Smart Response (Conversational)
“No problem — I’ll help clarify 👍
Contamination usually relates to past uses of the land that may have left harmful substances in the ground, such as:• Old industrial or factory use• Petrol stations or garages• Waste disposal or landfill• Chemical storage or heavy construction activity
👉 If your property has always been residential, contamination is unlikely.

AGENT X (ACTION LAYER)
Contamination Present:
Contamination: YesActions:🔴 Trigger Environmental Workflow✔ Assign environmental consultant✔ Order Phase 1 Report✔ Hold submission until cleared✔ Inform customer of risk & timeline
No Contamination:
Contamination: NoActions:✔ Proceed with standard workflow✔ No environmental restriction
Not Sure:
Contamination: UnknownActions:✔ Check land history records✔ Recommend environmental screening✔ Keep submission conditional

🤖 Agent Z Smart Response (Conversational)
“No problem — I’ll explain 👍
A Flood Risk Assessment (FRA) is a report that evaluates:• Flood risk to your property• Impact of your development• Drainage and mitigation measures
👉 This is usually required if your property is in Flood Zone 2 or 3.

FRA NOT Available (Zone 2/3):

Newham requires:
Interlinked smoke alarms on every storey

All alarms must be mainswired with battery backup

field: Site visit for verification Task for YTeam
text:
AGENT Z — Newham Council Ready Interpretation
Newham Council Compliance Logic
Newham requires an annual Gas Safety Certificate (CP12) for any property with:
A gas boiler
Gas hob/cooker
Gas fire
Any gas appliance
The certificate must be:
Issued within the last 12 months
Completed by a Gas Safe Registered Engineer
Uploaded as part of the HMO or planning compliance pack

AGENT Z — Newham Council Ready Interpretation
Newham Council Compliance Logic
Newham requires a valid Electrical Installation Condition Report (EICR) for all rented or HMO properties.
The EICR must:
Be issued within the last 5 years
Be completed by a qualified NICEIC or NAPIT electrician
Clearly state the outcome:
Satisfactory (acceptable)
Unsatisfactory (requires remedial work)
Include:
Circuit test results
Observations & recommendations
Remedial actions (if any)

Any remedial actions

Request:
Photo of fuse board

Provide cost estimate
Send booking link

Newham Council Compliance Logic
Newham requires an Energy Performance Certificate (EPC) for all rented properties and HMO applications.
The EPC must:
Be valid for 10 years
Show a rating of E or above (minimum legal standard)
Be issued by an accredited domestic energy assessor
Be included in the compliance pack

1. AGENT Z — Newham Council Ready Interpretation
Newham Council Compliance Logic
Newham requires that all HMOs and rental properties have:
A constant, potable (drinkable) water supply
Adequate cold and hot water to all fixtures
Water systems free from contamination
Compliance with Legionella risk management
No shared external taps as the primary supply
Sufficient pressure for all occupants

Request:

Leaks

Compliance cannot proceed until resolved.

This is a critical failure for Newham compliance.
Risk Level: Very High Triggers: ✔ Immediate escalation to Health & Safety Review ✔ Property cannot proceed for HMO or planning compliance ✔ Customer must arrange plumbing repair or reconnection ✔ If water supply is intermittent → treat as “NO”

Basic plumbing fix: £80 – £150
Boiler/hot water repair: £120 – £300
Cold water tank replacement: £300 – £600
Mains pressure booster: £250 – £450

Treat as nonverified until proven.

Kitchen tap running
Bathroom taps
Boiler / hot water cylinder
Cold water tank (if present) ✔ If unclear → Trigger Site Visit (Water Supply Check) ✔ Provide cost estimate

London Zones 1–4: £120 – £180
Greater London / M25: £100 – £150
Home Counties: £90 – £140

1. AGENT Z — Newham Council Ready Interpretation
Newham Council Compliance Logic
Newham requires all HMOs and rental properties to have:
A safe, functional connection to the public sewer or an approved private system
No blockages, leaks, or backflow issues

Kitchens

Major drain replacement: £1,000 – £3,000+

Manhole covers
External drainage

Gurgling noises
Foul smells ✔ If unclear → Trigger Site Visit (Drainage Assessment) ✔ Provide cost estimate

London Zones 1–4: £120 – £180
Greater London / M25: £100 – £150
Home Counties: £90 – £140

1. AGENT Z — Newham Council Ready Interpretation
Newham Council Compliance Logic
Newham requires all HMOs and rental properties to have adequate surface water drainage to prevent:
Flooding
Water pooling
Damp and mould
Structural damage
Neighbouring property impact
Surface water must drain into:
Public surface water sewer, OR
Soakaway system, OR
Sustainable Drainage System (SuDS)
It must NOT drain into the foul sewer unless explicitly permitted.

AGENT X — Action Instructions (Operational Layer)

Request:
Photos of gutters, downpipes, and drainage points
Confirmation of drainage type (soakaway / sewer / SuDS)
Any history of flooding or pooling
Update briefcase: “Surface water drainage confirmed — verification pending.”
Prepare redacted summary for Agent Y.
If customer answered NO
Inform customer:
Surface water drainage is mandatory for Newham compliance.
Compliance cannot proceed until resolved.
Provide repair cost guidance.
Recommend:
Drainage specialist
Gutter/downpipe repair
Soakaway installation (if needed)
Update briefcase: “Surface water drainage missing — urgent repair required.”
Prepare redacted summary for Agent Y.
If customer answered NOT SURE
Request:
Photos of external drainage
Photos of gutters/downpipes
Any signs of water pooling
If unclear → Trigger Site Visit (Drainage Assessment)
Provide cost estimate
Send booking link
Update briefcase: “Surface water drainage status unknown — awaiting verification / site visit.”
Prepare redacted summary for Agent Y.
If customer answers: NO
This is a major compliance issue.
Newham will not approve an HMO or planning application if:
Rainwater has nowhere to drain
Water pools near the building
Gutters/downpipes are missing or broken
Surface water drains into foul sewer illegally
Risk Level: Very High Triggers: ✔ Immediate drainage assessment ✔ Customer must arrange repair or installation ✔ Compliance paused until resolved
Typical Repair Costs (London & Surrounding Areas)
Gutter/downpipe repair: £80 – £150
New soakaway installation: £600 – £1,200
Surface water drain repair: £200 – £600
SuDS installation: £1,000 – £3,000+
If customer answers: NOT SURE
Treat as nonverified until confirmed.

Gutters
Downpipes
External ground levels
Any visible drainage grates ✔ Ask if there is any water pooling after rain ✔ If unclear → Trigger Site Visit (Drainage Assessment) ✔ Provide cost estimate
Standard Site Visit Cost
London Zones 1–4: £120 – £180
Greater London / M25: £100 – £150
Home Counties: £90 – £140

1. AGENT Z — Newham Council Ready Interpretation
Newham Council Compliance Logic
Newham requires all HMOs and rental properties to have:
Adequate waste storage capacity for the number of occupants
Correct bin types (general waste, recycling, food waste)
Secure, pestproof storage areas
Clear access routes for waste collection
No accumulation of rubbish in front or rear gardens
Compliance with Newham Waste & Recycling Standards

Any issues with overflow or pests
Check if bin capacity matches occupancy

They must order correct bins before proceeding.

Advice on creating a secure waste storage area

1. AGENT Z — Newham Council Ready Interpretation
Newham Council Planning & HMO Compliance Logic
Newham strongly supports renewable energy installations but requires proper classification and documentation, especially for HMOs or properties undergoing planning review.
Renewable systems include:
Solar PV panels
Solar thermal (hot water)
Air source heat pumps (ASHP)
Ground source heat pumps (GSHP)
Battery storage systems
Microwind turbines
EV charging points (lowcarbon infrastructure)

Location (roof, garden, external wall)
Photos of installation area
Trigger:
Planning classification review
Fire safety review (if battery storage or heat pump)
Update briefcase: “Renewable installation proposed — awaiting technical assessment.”
Prepare redacted summary for Agent Y.
If customer answered NO
No action required beyond recording the response.
Update briefcase: “No renewable installations proposed.”
Prepare redacted summary for Agent Y.

Request:
What type of system they are considering
Photos of roof/external areas
Offer:

✅ 1. AGENT Z — Newham Council Ready Interpretation
What “Additional Consents” Means in Newham
Newham Council may require extra consents depending on the property type, location, and proposed use. These may include:
Common Additional Consents
Freeholder Consent Required if the property is leasehold.
Mortgage Lender Consent Required if the property is mortgaged and being converted to HMO.
Building Control Approval Needed for structural changes, fire doors, partitions, bathrooms, etc.
Planning Permission / Lawful Development Certificate Required for:
Change of use (C3 → C4 or Sui Generis)
Extensions
Loft conversions
Outbuildings
External alterations
Party Wall Agreements Required if works affect shared walls or boundaries.
Conservation Area Consent If the property is in a conservation area.
Listed Building Consent If the property is listed.
Environmental Health Approval For large HMOs or properties with previous enforcement history.

AGENT X — Action Instructions (Operational Layer)
If customer answered YES
Request:
Type of consent(s) required
Whether consent has been obtained
Upload of any supporting documents
Trigger:
Planning/legal review
Freeholder/mortgage compliance check
Update briefcase: “Additional consents declared — verification pending.”
Prepare redacted summary for Agent Y.
If customer answered NO
No action required beyond recording the response.
Update briefcase: “No additional consents required.”
Prepare redacted summary for Agent Y.
If customer answered NOT SURE
Ask:
Is the property leasehold?
Is the property mortgaged?
Are any structural works planned?
Is the property in a conservation area?
Offer:
Consent Check Review
Provide cost estimate
Update briefcase: “Additional consents unknown — awaiting clarification / review.”
Prepare redacted summary for Agent Y.
